Article.ArticleTitle: Precision diagnostics for MBLs: the true game changer in treating antimicrobial resistance.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Instituto de Biología Molecular y Celular de Rosario, CONICET, Universidad Nacional de Rosario, Rosario, Argentina.

Department of Molecular Biology and Microbiology, Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine, Cleveland, OH, USA; CWRU-Cleveland VAMC Center for Antimicrobial Resistance and Epidemiology, Cleveland, OH, USA; Research Service, Veterans Affairs Northeast Ohio Healthcare System, Cleveland, OH, USA.

Instituto de Biología Molecular y Celular de Rosario, CONICET, Universidad Nacional de Rosario, Rosario, Argentina; Facultad de Ciencias Bioquímicas y Farmacéuticas, Universidad Nacional de Rosario, Rosario 2000, Argentina.

Department of Molecular Biology and Microbiology, Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine, Cleveland, OH, USA; Department of Medicine, Department of Biochemistry, Department of Pharmacology, and Department of Proteomics and Bioinformatics, Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine, Cleveland, OH, USA; CWRU-Cleveland VAMC Center for Antimicrobial Resistance and Epidemiology, Cleveland, OH, USA; Research Service, Veterans Affairs Northeast Ohio Healthcare System, Cleveland, OH, USA. Electronic address: robert.bonomo@va.gov.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: RAB and AJV had a research contract with Shionogi. RAB had research contracts with VenatoRx, Merck, Wockhardt, and Entasis. RAB has participated on data safety monitoring boards for phase 1 clinical studies evaluating intravenous apramycin and an antimicrobial peptide. AJV declares honoraria from Pfizer for a lecture delivered as part of a clinician training programme. All other authors declare no competing interests.
